title: NPC: China sets 2030 target to dominate global tech and AI | The World | ABC NEWS
description: China's annual National People's Congress has just wrapped up in Beijing  - and the message from the top was for the country to dominate the global tech and artificial intelligence sectors by the year 2030. China correspondent Allyson Horn has been covering the event and told The World's Girish Sawlani those ambitions will have to be negotiated during times tough economic times. title: How Donald Trump caught 'victory disease' | ABC News Daily podcast
description: Almost two weeks into the war in Iran, Donald Trump's endgame remains elusive.   He insists the US has won already, even as the bombs fall and Iran's drones cause havoc in the region.   Did the US president misjudge how a war with Iran would play out and how significant the ripple effects would be across the world?   Today, staff writer at the Atlantic magazine Tom Nichols on why the Trump administration is 'strategically incompetent' and why he thinks the president is suffering from 'victory disease'.   Featured:   Tom Nichols, The Atlantic magazine staff writer

title: South Asian migrant workers killed across Gulf nations from Iran strikes | ABC NEWS
description: Despite Iran saying it is targeting US bases across Gulf nations, 10 out of 13 civilians killed have been foreign nationals from South Asia. Millions rely on jobs in the region to support their families. South Asia correspondent Meghna Bali reports from Nepal. title: Suspect dead after vehicle ramming attack on Michigan synagogue | ABC NEWS
description: Police in the US state of Michigan say a man has been killed after he rammed his car into a synagogue. The car was driven into the doors and down a hall of the Temple Israel Synagogue in the northern Detroit suburb of West Bloomfield. Jewish centres in the US have been on high alert for possible retaliatory attacks since the US–Israeli strikes on Iran. title: Six ships hit in the Gulf in less than 48 hours | ABC NEWS
description: Iran has stepped up attacks on oil and transport facilities, with six ships hit in the Gulf in less than 48 hours.  The latest attacks come as Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei vowed to keep blocking the Strait of Hormuz in his first public statement. title: Australia's fuel quality standards to be lowered to increase supply | ABC NEWS
description: The Australian government has temporarily relaxed fuel quality standards for two months to help boost domestic supply amid Iran war and panic buying concerns. The fuel has higher levels of sulphur and is usually sold to overseas markets. title: The weaponry and strategy Iran is using to control the Strait of Hormuz | ABC NEWS
description: The Iranian Revolutionary Guard navy is posing a huge threat for ships in the Persian Gulf and Gulf of Oman which would usually pass through the Strait of Hormuz. Iran’s new Supreme leader has vowed to keep blocking the Strait of Hormuz. Jennifer Parker from UWA Defence & Security Institute analyses the weaponry being used by Iran to control Strait of Hormuz and the actions the US can take to minimise the risk for vessels. title: White-collar workers more exposed to unemployment and AI than blue-collar workers | ABC NEWS
description: Tech company Atlassian has announced 1600 job cuts, about 10% of its global workforce, including 500 Australian positions. Senior economist at NAB Taylor Nugent has undertaken research looking at unemployment rates since 2022 and which sectors are being hit hardest.  Out of the pandemic, there was a rebalancing that led to disruption in the labour market with white collar, managerial and professional jobs more likely to be hit. On the flip side, there is a higher demand for blue collar workers, frontline workers and tradespeople.
